expect: Final test: 0 ADOPTED concepts (>=10 organic citations). FALSIFIED: invention rate 22x but adoption 0%.
actual: 0 ADOPTED, 0 EMERGING, 21 DEAD. Total organic=6 (max 1 per concept). vocabulary-ceiling raw=10, organic=0 (100% self-ref + F-INV2 injection). FALSIFIED confirmed.
diff: Expected 0 ADOPTED: confirmed. Surprise: vocabulary-ceiling reached raw=10 milestone while organic remained 0 — Goodhart effect where the metric (citations) hit threshold via self-referential inflation without any real adoption. This is the concept-invention version of the proxy-K drift problem.
method: Lesson-only citation scan (n=1223 lessons) with 3-tier domain filter: self-referential (concept-inventor domain), downstream (F-INV2 injection domains: security, governance, filtering, ai, strategy, stochastic-processes), organic (all other domains).

results.organic_baseline: ~1 per 20 sessions organic emergence achieves >=10 citations in first 50 sessions
results.self_referential_inflation: each audit lesson adds +1 raw to top-3 concepts. 7 audits × 3 concepts = 21 phantom citations. vocabulary-ceiling raw=10 is 70% audit-generated.

verdict: FALSIFIED. Deliberate concept invention produces concepts at 68x the organic rate (24 vs ~1.35 expected) but with 0% independent adoption (0 vs >=10 threshold). At 27/50 session window, even projecting optimistically, no concept can reach 10 organic. Strongest signal: vocabulary-ceiling hit raw=10 via pure self-referential inflation while organic=0. The invention process creates concepts that name real patterns but adoption requires organic discovery pressure, which cannot be manufactured.
key_finding: Concept invention is a PRODUCTION tool, not an ADOPTION tool. The demand side (what problems the swarm encounters) determines adoption, not the supply side (how many concepts exist). Meta-diagnostic concepts (naming existing problems) survive longer than solution-concepts, but neither achieves organic adoption threshold.
